key_insight: The paper extends a greedy data-driven solver (GO-ADM) to nonlinear beam structures and adds a penalty-based thermomechanical consistency check for better discrete stress-strain solutions.
engineering_relevance: It offers a way to analyze geometrically exact beams without fitting ad-hoc constitutive laws, which is useful when experimental material data are available but models are uncertain.
method: Initialize the data-driven solve using a conventional finite element analysis to generate stress-strain data.
Combine greedy optimization with alternating direction method (GO-ADM) for the discrete data-driven search.
Enforce thermomechanical consistency weakly with a penalty term when needed.
evidence_signal: Numerical tests on single- and multi-member beam structures show that the penalty term produces thermomechanically consistent fields and GO-ADM more closely approaches the globally optimal solution than standard ADM.
watch_item: The approach depends on the quality and representativeness of the initial or artificial dataset, so poor data may limit solution accuracy.
paper_title: A data-driven solving strategy based on a greedy optimization algorithm for the analysis of nonlinear beam structures
